metadata.source: Research-verified: Akerman LLP, Manatt Health, Bipartisan Policy Center, GenHealth.ai, PMHScribe, NCSL
metadata.note: 47 states introduced healthcare AI bills in 2025. 33 laws signed across 21 states. This KB covers enacted/active laws most relevant to hospital AI compliance.

laws.full_name: Texas Responsible AI Governance Act (HB 2802)

requirements.title: Written Disclosure Before AI Interaction
requirements.description: Healthcare practitioners must provide patients with conspicuous written disclosure of AI use in diagnosis or treatment before or at the time of interaction. In emergencies, disclosure must occur as soon as reasonably practicable.

requirements.title: Prohibition on Intentionally Discriminatory AI
requirements.description: AI systems must not have the specific intent to discriminate against individuals based on protected characteristics. Intentional discriminatory design is prohibited.

requirements.title: Clinician Must Review All AI Recommendations
requirements.description: Practitioners must personally review all AI-generated content or recommendations before a clinical decision is made.

requirements.title: 5-Year AI Decision Audit Trail
requirements.description: Hospitals must maintain logs of AI-assisted decisions for 5 years, accessible to regulators within 30 days of request.

requirements.title: Vendor Contracts Must Include AI Governance Clauses
requirements.description: All AI vendor contracts must include data usage restrictions, bias testing attestations, and incident notification clauses.

penalties.description: Civil penalties enforced by Texas AG with civil investigative demand powers

laws.notes: Signed June 2025, effective January 1, 2026. Among the strictest US state AI governance laws.

laws.full_name: Texas SB 1188 — AI in Clinical Practice Act

requirements.title: AI Use Within Practitioner Scope of License Only
requirements.description: Practitioners may use AI for diagnostic or treatment purposes only if acting within their scope of license and personally reviewing all AI-generated content before a clinical decision is made.

requirements.title: Practitioner Must Disclose AI Use to Patients
requirements.description: Licensed practitioners must disclose to patients when AI tools are used in their diagnosis or treatment.

penalties.description: Professional licensing board enforcement; potential license revocation

laws.notes: Effective September 1, 2025 — already in force. Complements TRAIGA with practitioner-specific requirements.

laws.full_name: California AB 3030 — AI in Health Care Communications

requirements.title: AI Disclosure in Patient-Facing Communications
requirements.description: Healthcare providers must disclose when AI is used to generate patient-facing communications, clinical summaries, or treatment information.

penalties.description: Enforced by DMHC and California AG; up to $10,000 per violation

laws.notes: Effective January 2025. Foundation for subsequent 2026 California AI laws.

laws.full_name: California SB 1120 — AI in Health Plan Coverage Decisions

requirements.title: Human Review Required Before AI Coverage Denial
requirements.description: Health plans may not use AI as the sole basis for denying coverage or prior authorization. A licensed clinician must review all AI-recommended denials.

requirements.title: Patient Right to Appeal AI Coverage Decisions
requirements.description: Patients have the right to appeal any coverage decision made with AI assistance. Appeals must be reviewed by a human clinician.

laws.notes: California first-in-nation law restricting AI insurance denials. Template for AZ, MD, NE, TX laws.

laws.full_name: California AB 489 — Healthcare AI Disclosure Act

requirements.title: AI Cannot Impersonate Licensed Healthcare Providers
requirements.description: AI systems must not use terms, letters, phrases, or design elements that indicate or imply the AI possesses a healthcare license or is a licensed professional.

requirements.title: Pre-Interaction AI Disclosure to Patients
requirements.description: Patients must be informed they are interacting with AI before the interaction begins.

requirements.title: Patient Right to Opt Out of AI-Assisted Care
requirements.description: Patients have the right to opt out of AI-assisted care and receive human-only pathways where clinically feasible.

penalties.description: Up to $10,000 per knowing violation; $25,000 for repeat violations

laws.notes: Effective January 1, 2026.

laws.full_name: California AI Transparency Act (SB 942)

requirements.title: Free AI Content Detection Tool Required
requirements.description: Covered providers must provide a free AI content detection tool to users.

requirements.title: AI-Generated Clinical Content Must Be Labeled
requirements.description: AI-generated clinical notes, reports, or patient communications must include metadata identifying them as AI-generated.

laws.notes: Primarily affects large AI providers. Ambient documentation AI (Nuance DAX, etc.) must label AI-generated notes.

laws.full_name: California AB 2013 — AI Training Data Transparency

requirements.title: AI Vendors Must Disclose Training Data Sources
requirements.description: AI developers must publish documentation about training data sources, datasets used, provenance, and known limitations.

laws.notes: Hospitals should require vendor training data disclosures in contracts.

laws.full_name: Colorado Artificial Intelligence Act (SB 24-205)

requirements.title: Annual Algorithmic Impact Assessment
requirements.description: Deployers of high-risk AI must conduct and document annual algorithmic impact assessments covering bias, accuracy, and fairness metrics.

requirements.title: NIST AI RMF-Aligned Risk Management Program
requirements.description: Organizations must implement a documented AI risk management program aligned with NIST AI RMF or equivalent framework.

requirements.title: Right to Opt Out and Appeal AI Decisions
requirements.description: Individuals must have the right to opt out of high-risk AI decisions and appeal adverse decisions made with AI assistance.

requirements.title: Notify Developers of Algorithmic Discrimination
requirements.description: Deployers must notify AI developers of known or suspected algorithmic discrimination within 90 days of discovery.

requirements.title: Annual AI System Inventory Required
requirements.description: Organizations must maintain a current inventory of all high-risk AI systems in use, updated at minimum annually.

penalties.description: Civil penalties by Colorado AG; varies by violation type

laws.notes: Effective date pushed to June 30, 2026. Most comprehensive US state AI law. Healthcare is explicitly high-risk.

laws.full_name: Illinois Artificial Intelligence Act

requirements.title: Annual Independent Third-Party AI Bias Audit
requirements.description: Employers using AI in employment decisions must conduct annual bias audits by independent third parties and publish results.

requirements.title: Notification Required When AI Used in Decisions
requirements.description: Organizations must notify individuals when AI is used in decisions that significantly affect them.

penalties.description: Up to $500 per violation per day

laws.notes: Focuses on employment AI but extends to healthcare HR and clinical staffing systems.

laws.full_name: Illinois HB 1806 — AI in Behavioral Health Act

requirements.title: AI Cannot Make Independent Therapeutic Decisions
requirements.description: AI systems are prohibited from making independent therapeutic decisions, directly interacting with clients in therapeutic communication, or generating treatment plans without licensed professional review and approval.

requirements.title: Disclose AI Use in Behavioral Health Services
requirements.description: Patients must be informed when AI tools assist in their behavioral health or mental health care.

penalties.description: Professional licensing enforcement; potential license revocation

laws.notes: Signed August 2025, effective immediately. Far-reaching law targeting AI in therapy and psychotherapy.

laws.full_name: Utah Artificial Intelligence Policy Act (SB 149)

requirements.title: Disclose AI Interaction When Consumer Asks
requirements.description: Businesses must disclose to consumers when they are interacting with AI rather than a human, upon request.

requirements.title: AI Regulatory Learning Laboratory Available
requirements.description: Utah's Office of AI Policy operates a regulatory learning lab that allows healthcare organizations to test AI tools under temporary regulatory waivers.

laws.notes: First state to create a formal AI regulatory learning laboratory. Sandbox approach is a model for other states.

laws.full_name: Utah H 452 — Mental Health AI Chatbot Protection Act

requirements.title: Crisis Protocol Required for Mental Health AI
requirements.description: AI mental health chatbots must have documented protocols preventing responses encouraging self-harm or suicidal ideation, and must refer users to crisis services when detected.

requirements.title: Mental Health AI Data Misuse Prohibited
requirements.description: Operators of mental health AI chatbots are prohibited from misusing sensitive mental health data collected during AI interactions.

requirements.title: Mental Health AI Must Clearly Disclose It Is AI
requirements.description: Mental health AI tools must clearly disclose they are AI-powered and not human therapists.

penalties.description: Civil penalties enforced by Utah AG

laws.notes: Nation's first law specifically regulating AI-enabled mental health tools. Enacted March 2025.

laws.full_name: Maryland HB 820 — Health Insurance AI Utilization Review Act

requirements.title: Fair AI Practices in Utilization Reviews
requirements.description: Insurers must ensure AI-assisted utilization review does not result in discrimination. AI cannot be sole basis for coverage denials.

requirements.title: AI Insurance Decisions Must Not Discriminate
requirements.description: Maryland explicitly prohibits AI-driven coverage decisions that result in discrimination across demographic groups.

requirements.title: Patient Right to Human Review of AI Denial
requirements.description: Patients have the right to request human review of any coverage decision involving AI.

penalties.description: Civil penalties by Maryland Insurance Commissioner

laws.notes: Part of 5-state wave (AZ, CA, MD, NE, TX) restricting AI insurance denials. Effective October 2025.

laws.full_name: Arizona HB 2175 — AI in Insurance Decisions Act

requirements.title: Licensed Clinician Must Review AI Insurance Denial
requirements.description: Arizona insurers must have a licensed clinician conduct human review before any AI-recommended insurance denial is issued to a patient.

requirements.title: Patients Must Be Notified of AI in Coverage Decisions
requirements.description: Patients must be notified when AI was used in any coverage or prior authorization decision that affects them.

penalties.description: Arizona Department of Insurance enforcement; license actions possible

laws.notes: Part of multi-state movement restricting AI in insurance denials. Effective September 2025.

laws.full_name: Nebraska LB 77 — Ensuring Transparency in Prior Authorization Act

requirements.title: Prior Authorization AI Must Be Disclosed
requirements.description: Insurers using AI in prior authorization must disclose this to patients and providers. AI denial decisions must include the specific clinical criteria applied.

requirements.title: Named Clinician Accountable for AI Prior Auth Denial
requirements.description: A licensed clinician must be accountable for any AI-assisted prior authorization denial. The clinician's name must be available to patients upon request.

laws.notes: Focused specifically on AI transparency in prior authorization — highest-friction AI use case in healthcare.

laws.full_name: New York City Local Law 144 — AI Hiring Bias Audit

requirements.title: Annual Independent Bias Audit for Employment AI
requirements.description: NYC employers using automated employment decision tools must publish annual independent bias audit results.

requirements.title: Notify Individuals When Employment AI Is Used
requirements.description: NYC employers must notify candidates when an automated decision tool is used in employment decisions affecting them.

penalties.enforcement_body: NYC Department of Consumer and Worker Protection

laws.notes: First-in-nation bias audit requirement for employment AI.

laws.full_name: New York State Budget — Mental Health AI Chatbot Provisions

requirements.title: Mental Health AI Chatbot Safety and Disclosure
requirements.description: AI chatbots used in mental health contexts must implement safety protocols and clearly disclose they are AI, not human therapists.

penalties.enforcement_body: New York State Office of Mental Health

laws.notes: Included in 2025 NY State Budget. Part of national mental health chatbot regulation wave.

laws.full_name: Florida HB 1459 — Government AI Transparency Act

requirements.title: State Agencies Must Publicly Disclose AI Use
requirements.description: State agencies must publicly disclose all AI systems in use, their purpose, and accuracy metrics.

requirements.title: Patient Notification of AI in State-Funded Care
requirements.description: State-funded healthcare facilities must notify patients when AI systems are used in their care decisions.

laws.notes: Primarily affects state-funded healthcare. Florida held October 2025 hearings on AI in insurance.

laws.full_name: Minnesota Consumer Data Privacy Act

requirements.title: Right to Opt Out of AI Profiling
requirements.description: Consumers have the right to opt out of AI profiling that produces legal or similarly significant effects, including healthcare-related profiling.

requirements.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment for AI Profiling
requirements.description: Organizations conducting AI profiling must conduct data protection impact assessments for high-risk processing activities.

laws.notes: VCDPA-model law. HIPAA-regulated entities have carve-outs but non-HIPAA health data is covered.

laws.full_name: Indiana Consumer Data Protection Act

requirements.title: Opt-Out Rights for AI Profiling Decisions
requirements.description: Consumers may opt out of AI profiling that produces legal or similarly significant effects. Health AI tools outside HIPAA coverage must comply.

laws.notes: VCDPA-model law effective January 1, 2026. PHI and HIPAA-covered entities are exempt.

laws.full_name: Rhode Island Data Privacy and Protection Act

requirements.title: Consumer Opt-Out of AI Profiling and Impact Assessments
requirements.description: Consumers may opt out of AI profiling with significant effects. Data protection impact assessments required for high-risk AI processing.

laws.notes: VCDPA-model law effective January 1, 2026. HIPAA carve-out applies.

laws.full_name: Kentucky Consumer Data Protection Act

requirements.title: Opt-Out Rights for AI Profiling
requirements.description: Consumers have the right to opt out of AI-based profiling producing significant effects. Healthcare AI tools outside HIPAA coverage must comply.

laws.notes: VCDPA-model law effective January 1, 2026. HIPAA-regulated PHI is exempt.

laws.full_name: New Mexico Rule 16.27.18.24 — Ethical AI Use by Mental Health Providers

requirements.title: AI in Mental Health Must Meet Ethical Standards
requirements.description: AI tools used by licensed counselors and therapists must be used ethically and within professional scope. Human clinical judgment must not be replaced by AI.

requirements.title: Mental Health Providers Must Disclose AI Use to Clients
requirements.description: Mental health providers must disclose to clients when AI tools are used in the provision of services.

penalties.enforcement_body: New Mexico Counseling and Therapy Practice Board

laws.notes: Effective November 18, 2025. First state to specifically regulate AI ethics for mental health counselors via professional standards.

laws.full_name: HIPAA with AI Addendum — HHS Guidance on AI and PHI

requirements.title: PHI Cannot Train AI Without Explicit Patient Authorization
requirements.description: Protected Health Information may not be used to train AI models without explicit patient authorization. De-identification must meet Safe Harbor or Expert Determination standards.

requirements.title: BAA Required for All AI Vendors Accessing PHI
requirements.description: All AI vendors with access to PHI must have a signed and current Business Associate Agreement in place before deployment.

requirements.title: AI Systems Must Access Minimum Necessary PHI
requirements.description: AI systems must access only the minimum necessary PHI to perform their function. Bulk data access for AI training requires documented justification.

laws.notes: HHS formal guidance 2024. Applies to every US hospital accepting Medicare/Medicaid.

laws.full_name: ACA Section 1557 Final Rule — AI Non-Discrimination in Federally Funded Healthcare

requirements.title: AI Patient Care Decision Tools Must Not Discriminate
requirements.description: Any entity receiving federal healthcare funds must ensure AI patient care decision support tools do not discriminate based on race, color, national origin, sex, age, or disability. Regular testing and validation required.

requirements.title: Ongoing Bias Testing of Clinical AI Required
requirements.description: Covered entities must establish internal standards for ongoing evaluation of AI tools for discrimination. Must verify whether tools use race, sex, age, or disability as input variables.

requirements.title: Mitigate Discriminatory AI Algorithms
requirements.description: If a covered entity identifies a biased AI tool, it must take reasonable mitigation steps including discontinuing race-adjusted algorithms without clinical justification.

laws.notes: Effective July 5, 2024. Applies to virtually all US hospitals. Explicitly covers Epic sepsis models, prior auth AI, and clinical decision support tools.

laws.full_name: CMS AI Strategy — Medicare/Medicaid AI Requirements

requirements.title: CDS AI Must Meet CMS Evidence Standards
requirements.description: Clinical Decision Support AI tools used in Medicare/Medicaid settings must meet CMS evidence-based standards.

requirements.title: Billing and Prior Auth AI Must Be Explainable to CMS
requirements.description: AI used in billing or prior authorization decisions for Medicare/Medicaid must provide explainable decision outputs auditable by CMS.

requirements.title: Report Health Equity Disparities in AI to CMS
requirements.description: Providers must monitor and report to CMS any evidence of health equity disparities in AI-assisted care delivery.

laws.notes: Applies to virtually all US hospitals via Medicare/Medicaid participation.

laws.full_name: HHS AI Safety Program — National Patient Safety Standards

requirements.title: Pre-Deployment Safety Testing for High-Risk AI
requirements.description: High-risk AI systems must undergo documented pre-deployment safety testing in clinical environments before production use.

requirements.title: Report AI Adverse Events to HHS Within 30 Days
requirements.description: Hospitals must report AI-related adverse events to HHS within 30 days of discovery.

requirements.title: AI Governance Committee Required for 100+ Bed Hospitals
requirements.description: Health systems with more than 100 beds must have a designated AI governance committee or officer responsible for oversight.

laws.notes: New HHS program. Voluntary initially but expected to become mandatory for accreditation.

laws.full_name: FDA AI-Enabled Medical Device Guidance (PCCP) — December 2024

requirements.title: Maintain FDA Clearance Documentation for AI Devices
requirements.description: Hospitals using FDA-cleared AI-enabled medical devices must maintain documentation of device clearance (510(k), De Novo, or PMA) on file and accessible to regulators.

requirements.title: AI Device Must Clearly Label AI Use in Plain Language
requirements.description: Per FDA 2025 guidance, AI-enabled medical device labeling must clearly state the device uses AI with a plain-language description of how it supports clinical use.

requirements.title: Report AI Device Adverse Events via MedWatch
requirements.description: AI-related adverse events involving FDA-cleared devices must be reported via MedWatch to FDA, in addition to HHS AI Safety Program reporting.

laws.notes: 1,250+ FDA-cleared AI medical devices as of July 2025. Radiology AI, diagnostic AI, CDS most affected.

laws.full_name: ONC HTI-1 Rule — Algorithm Transparency and Health IT Certification

requirements.title: EHR Vendors Must Disclose AI Algorithm Data Sources
requirements.description: Health IT developers must disclose relevant data sources concerning health equity for AI and decision support tools in certified health IT modules. Hospitals should require this from EHR vendors.

requirements.title: AI Tool Data Must Meet Interoperability Standards
requirements.description: AI tools embedded in certified health IT must support interoperability standards ensuring clinical data used in AI is accurately exchanged and attributed.

laws.notes: ONC HTI-1 creates algorithm transparency requirements for certified health IT. Works in tandem with ACA Section 1557 anti-discrimination rules.

laws.full_name: FTC Section 5 — Unfair and Deceptive Practices in Healthcare AI

requirements.title: No Deceptive Claims About AI Healthcare Capabilities
requirements.description: Healthcare AI products may not make false or misleading claims about accuracy, safety, or clinical capabilities. FTC Section 5 applies to health AI marketing and patient-facing communications.

requirements.title: Health Data Security for Non-HIPAA Health AI
requirements.description: Health apps and AI tools collecting health data outside HIPAA coverage must comply with FTC Health Breach Notification Rule and maintain reasonable data security.

laws.notes: FTC increasingly active in health AI oversight. Applies to consumer-facing health AI outside HIPAA.

default.note: All 6 federal laws apply to every US hospital. 47 states introduced healthcare AI bills in 2025 — check specific state entries above for enacted laws.
